channel.title: NPR Topics: Health
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: What is an aortic dissection?
item.description: NPR's A Martinez speaks with American Heart Association's volunteer president Dr. Manesh Patel about aortic dissection, the preliminary finding of what led to Sen. Lindsey Graham's death.
item.content:encoded: NPR's A Martinez speaks with American Heart Association's volunteer president Dr. Manesh Patel about aortic dissection, the preliminary finding of what led to Sen. Lindsey Graham's death.
item.dc:creator: A Martínez

item.title: Trump's HHS abandons threat to withhold Medicare and Medicaid funding over trans care
item.description: NPR has learned that the Department of Health and Human Services will not be finalizing its most aggressive attempt to end gender-affirming care for youth nationally.
item.content:encoded:
NPR has learned that the Department of Health and Human Services will not be finalizing its most aggressive attempt to end gender-affirming care for youth nationally.

(Image credit: Erik McGregor)
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin

item.title: Pythons' extreme biology may hold clues for treating human disease
item.description: These snakes can go for months without eating, grow and shrink the size of their hearts and jump start their metabolism on a dime.
item.content:encoded:
These snakes can go for months without eating, grow and shrink the size of their hearts and jump start their metabolism on a dime.

(Image credit: Ari Daniel for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Ari Daniel

item.title: Eat your ice cream for a long, healthy life? This doctor says so
item.description: Dr. Zeke Emanuel, a renowned U.S. health policy physician, takes on the wellness industry with a back-to-basics approach to optimizing good health.
item.content:encoded:
Dr. Zeke Emanuel, a renowned U.S. health policy physician, takes on the wellness industry with a back-to-basics approach to optimizing good health.

(Image credit: WeBond Creations)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: What to know about the cyclosporiasis outbreak hitting more than half of U.S. states
item.description: A surge of cases of the intestinal illness that causes diarrhea and nausea has been detected in 31 states, according to federal health authorities, but the source is still under investigation.
item.content:encoded:
A surge of cases of the intestinal illness that causes diarrhea and nausea has been detected in 31 states, according to federal health authorities, but the source is still under investigation.

(Image credit: Ron Harris)
item.dc:creator: Joe Hernandez

item.title: Influencers are questioning common guidance about breast milk. Here's what science says
item.description: The guidance over leftover breast milk says toss it after 2 hours. But a recent preprint study shared on social media has some people questioning that advice.
item.content:encoded: The guidance over leftover breast milk says toss it after 2 hours. But a recent preprint study shared on social media has some people questioning that advice.
item.dc:creator: Maria Godoy

item.title: A promising tale from Senegal of fish, rice .... and dangerous worms
item.description: Farmers in Senegal are welcoming fish into their rice paddies. The hope is they'll fertilize the crop, be a source of food ... and eat the snails that carry parasitic worms.
item.content:encoded:
Farmers in Senegal are welcoming fish into their rice paddies. The hope is they'll fertilize the crop, be a source of food ... and eat the snails that carry parasitic worms.

(Image credit: Ricci Shyrock for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Jonathan Lambert

item.title: Most people who need glasses don't have them. Can the post office change that?
item.description: In some towns in India, a visitor to the post office who's squinting at fine print might be asked: Do you want an eye test?
item.content:encoded:
In some towns in India, a visitor to the post office who's squinting at fine print might be asked: Do you want an eye test?

(Image credit: Subhamoy Bhattacharjee for NPR)
item.dc:creator: Gabrielle Emanuel

item.title: Cyclospora outbreaks grow in the Midwest
item.description: Michigan reports 1,250 cases of cyclospora and CDC surveillance shows cases in 17 states. The CDC has initiated a traceback investigation, as the source is not yet known.
item.content:encoded: Michigan reports 1,250 cases of cyclospora and CDC surveillance shows cases in 17 states. The CDC has initiated a traceback investigation, as the source is not yet known.
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Taliban declares war on smartphones
item.description: A newly announced ban on smartphones for government workers, police and military personnel is spilling over into healthcare and educational facilities. Ordinary citizens worry they'll be next.
item.content:encoded:
A newly announced ban on smartphones for government workers, police and military personnel is spilling over into healthcare and educational facilities. Ordinary citizens worry they'll be next.

(Image credit: Wakil Kohsar/AFP)
item.dc:creator: Fatima Faizi
